submission.questionnaireText:
Brett Burton
Adult 04/19/1979 (46)  Schedule appointment  Edit

1. Initial Health Survey (complete at your earliest convenience)
09/16/2025 at 3:03 PM
Note



1. Do you gain weight easily?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
2. [IF SO] Where do you typically gain weight on your body?
Around the belly
3. Do you feel "puffy," i.e your fat tends to be soft and feel like water retention?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
4. Do you struggle with unintended weight loss?
(1) I do not experience this
5. [WOMEN ONLY] Do you accumulate cellulite?
Not Applicable
6. Do you struggle with low appetite?
(1) I do not experience this
7. Do you tend to wake up when sleeping?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
8. If you tend to wake up when sleeping, is it: within the first few hours of falling asleep OR later on in the night OR both?
Later in the night
9. If you tend to wake up when sleeping, is it to: urinate OR eat something OR drink something OR seemingly no reason?
Seemingly no reason
10. If you tend to wake up when sleeping, do you find that you are hot or sweaty upon waking?
Yes
11. Do you NOT dream?
(1) I do not experience this
12. Do you experience nightmares or sleep paralysis?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
13. Do you have trouble falling asleep?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
14. Do you feel like you DON'T get a restful sleep?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
15. Do you frequently feel cold, especially at the extremities (fingertips, toes, and nose)?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
16. [WOMEN ONLY] Do you frequently have painful menstrual cramps?
Not Applicable
17. [WOMEN ONLY] Do you frequently have irregular menstruation or suffer from premenstrual syndrome?
Not Applicable
18. Do you suffer from chronic congestion without mucus?
(1) I do not experience this
19. Do you frequently experience mucus buildup in the throat or nose?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
20. [IF SO] Is it exacerbated after eating?
Yes
21. Do you suffer from dental problems such as bad breath, cavities, and gum inflammation?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
22. Do you often find that you are irritable or easily agitated?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
23. Do you suffer from "brain fog," low energy or feel like you do not have mental clarity?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
24. Do you tend to have an "afternoon crash" after eating lunch?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
25. Do you often feel "hyper aware," jittery, or restless?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
26. Do you notice the sensation of your heart pounding or difficulty breathing?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
27. Have you had a loss of pleasure in activities you normally enjoy?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
28. Have you noticed a lack of sexual interest or desire?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
29. [MEN ONLY] Have you lost morning erections?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
30. Do you have skin issues such as dry skin, eczema, or acne?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
31. Can you trace a worsening of your symptoms back to a traumatic event?
No
32. Do you have specific food cravings? If so, please list the foods you crave.
Yes
Crave sugar often in the afternoon, and salty items frequently in the evening
33. Do you have a history of mental health issues? If so, please list them.
No
34. Do you experience constipation?
(1) I do not experience this
35. Do you experience nausea?
(1) I do not experience this
36. Do you experience bloating (subjective feeling)?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
37. Do you experience distention (visible expansion of the abdomen)?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
38. Do you experience diarrhea?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
39. Do you experience belching?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
40. Do you experience flatulence?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
41. Do you experience pain in your upper abdomen?
(1) I do not experience this
42. Do you experience pain in your right upper quadrant?
(1) I do not experience this
43. Do you experience pain in your lower abdomen?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
44. Are you aware of any specific foods that trigger your symptoms? If so, please list them.
No
45. Do you experience dry stools?
(1) I do not experience this
46. Do you experience acid reflux?
(1) I do not experience this
47. Do your symptoms worsen IMMEDIATELY after eating?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
48. Do your symptoms worsen 1-3 hours after eating?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
49. Do symptoms alleviate after a bowel movement?
Sometimes
50. Have you tried anything in the past to alleviate your symptoms? These can be dietary changes, supplements, prescriptions or anything else. If so, please list them, and how helpful (or unhelpful) they were for you. Please be as specific as possible, including specific foods and brands and dosages of supplements, as well as what symptoms they affected.
Yes
Was diagnosed with H Pylori via GI-Map stool testing. Previously my GI symptoms of gas, bloating, and diarrhea were severe. Have undergone several rounds of antibiotics and supplementation and my GI symptoms have improved, less gas and less bloat. But severe brain fog, especially after eating protein and/or fats, still remains. Still have low energy issues as well.
51. Are you currently taking any supplements? If so, please list them and be as specific as possible, including the brand and dosage of any supplement. Please mention how helpful (or unhelpful) you perceive them to be.
Yes
Doctor's Best Zinc L-Carnosine, 2 capsules, twice a day with food: Seems to help keep GI symptoms under control
Sports Research Vitamin K2, 45 mcg, once a day with breakfast
Naturelo Vitamin C, 500mg, once a day with breakfast
Thorne Vitamin D, 5,000 IU, once a day with breakfast
Thorne Copper Bisglycinate, 2mg, once every other day with breakfast
Thorne Calcium Magnesium Malate, 2 x 100mg capsules, once a day with breakfast, helps with energy
Con-Cret Creatine HCL Capsules, 1.5g at breakfast and 1.5g at lunch: Helps with energy and brain fog. Too much creatine makes my sleep noticeably worse.
Designs for Health Vitamin E, 300mg, once a day with breakfast: Seems to help with thyroid function (increased body temp)
Now Vitamin B-1, 100mg, once a day with breakfast: Helps with energy
Thorne Vit B3 Niacinaide, 500mg, once a day with breakfast: Helps with energy
Thorne Vit B6 P-5-P, 33.8mg, once a day with breakfast: Helps with energy
Life Extension Vit B7 Biotin, 600mg, once a day with breakfast: Helps with energy
Notes:
I have tried all types of magnesium and have trouble absorbing all of them. This one is the best I can tolerate, but more then this dosage and I have diarrhea issues.
My GI tract also cannot handle very high Vitamin B complex supplements, especially those high in B12. I've had much better results with the individual ones I'm currently using.
52. Are you currently taking any medications? If so, please list them and be as specific as possible, including the brand and dosage of any medication.
Yes
Levothyroxine, 100 mcg, once per day before breakfast
Liothronine, 10 mcg before breakfast, 10mcg at lunch, 10mcg at dinner (30mcg total per day)
Testosterone Cypionate, 50mg IM injection, twice per week
53. Are you currently diagnosed with any medical conditions? If so, please list them.
Yes
Hashimoto's thyroiditis
Low testosterone
54. Please describe your diet. What foods do you typically eat, and in what amounts? Are these foods prepared by you, or are they pre-packaged, or from a restaurant? At what times do you typically eat?
7AM breakfast: 2 Eggs and/or some light carbs, such as a croissant

12PM lunch: Typically a chicken salad

6PM dinner: Balanced with lean protein such as steak, chicken, carbs such as white rice or potatoes, vegetables such as carrots or broccoli

I prepare almost all my own food.

55. Please describe your lifestyle. Do you get outside often? Do you exercise? How stressful is your day-to-day life due to work or other factors?
Walk or ride bikes outside 2 times per week

Do a medium intensity weighlifting workout 2-3 times per week

Daily work is typically stressful and often work long days

56. What are your health primary goals you are looking achieve?
Need to cure my brain fog as the #1 priority, it's affecting my ability to perform my job

Need to increase my energy as the #2 priority, it's affecting my quality of life with my family

57. Have you ever had food poisoning before? If so, when?
I have had several GI issues and sensitivities to many foods that cause Diarrhea over the years. It's hard to know which ones were caused by actual food poisoning, but I'm sure I've had it before in the past.

58. Please list your height, weight and waist measurements.
6'3" tall

Currently 215lbs

Waist 37 inches

59. Please list any other information you think would help us help you.
Have struggled with GI issues since college (1998-2004). Was able to avoid foods that caused issues and see improvement for many years after graduating. Was in peak physical condition in 2017, then had a bad sinus infection in that took a few rounds of antibiotics to cure. My son was also born at the same time. The lack of sleep from being a new parent, combined with the sinus infections/antibiotics, seemed to set me on my currently list of issues and I haven't been able to get back to my old self since.

I was diagnosed with low testosterone in 2013 (age 34) and have been on hormone replacement therapy since. I was very long on energy then and this helped for many years. But even now it's seems to just barely help me get by.

My body temperate is improved with the thyroid medications, but it seems like I always need to keep increasing my dosage to get the same affects.

I can fall asleep fine but typically wake up after only 4-5 hours, I feel hot and itchy when I do.

I have tried every probiotic. Many of them help to stabilize my GI issues (less diarrhea, more regular bowel movements, less gas) but they also noticeably make my brain fog much worse.

A low carb diet improves my joint pain/inflammation, and helps me to lean out, but it makes the brain fog and energy levels worse.

Eating higher carbs and lower fat/protein decreases brain fog and helps energy, but my joint pain/inflammation is almost unbearable.

I retain a lot of fat and fluid around my face, even when the rest of my body is relatively lean.

I have a white tongue, I have some dermatitis behind my ears and in my ear canal, that seemed like it could be fungal. An oral swab for Candida was negative and I've tried clotrimazole and it didn't improve those symptoms.
submission.takehomeText:
What are your four resting heart beats per minute? Please separate values with commas. [example: 78, 81, 75, 80]
72,70,70,74

How many times did you urinate each day for four days? Please separate values with commas. [example: 6, 6, 8, 7]
5,7,6,6 (Very foamy first thing in the morning, not very foamy later in the day)

On average, how foamy was your urine?
(3) Moderately foamy
How many times did you defecate per day for four days? Please separate values with commas. [example: 3, 1, 0, 2]
2,1,2,1

What Types on the Bristol Stool Scale did each of your bowel movements fall under? Please separate values with commas. [example: Type 2, Type 2, Type 4, Type 5, Type 7, Type 1, Type 6]
Please describe any other abnormalities of the stool observed, such as altered color or undigested food in the stool.
Day 1: Type 4, Type 5, Day 2: Type 4, Day 3: Type 4, Type 6, Day 4: Type 4

What were both of your temperature measurements each morning? Please separate values with commas. [example:
98.2, 98.4]

96.7, 96.7, 96.5, 96.4 (all measured under the armpit)

[WOMEN ONLY] What date did your last period begin? [MEN: SKIP QUESTION]
No answer given.

[WOMEN ONLY] What date did you take your first temperature measurement? [MEN: SKIP QUESTION]
No answer given.
submission.advisorNotesText:
Appointment #1
With: Shannon Sirois 
Billing code: 
consulting
Edit
5:00 PM – 6:00 PM
Progress Note 
This was a very interesting case. This is what I would say is a classic example of something that was mismanaged for years by the medical system.

Brett suffered from viral meningitis when he was a child, and spent a significant amount of time in the hospital where they stuffed him full of antibiotics, anti-virals, meds, etc. He was in his early teens and states that after this episode, he’s never been the same. He lost all of his muscle mass and his athletic performance, strength and endurance. Prior to this illness he was athletic and after the illness he said not only was his energy never the same, his coordination was also limited. I’m really curious if they used fluoroquinolones in his treatment and he never recovered from being floxxed. He had no idea what was used.

Brett is an engineer, so he has tried to figure this out on his own. He’s tried just about everything, no joke, so I’ll try to streamline this so we only get the pertinent information. Low carb immediately gets rid of his joint pain, but his brain fog and energy are in the tank. High carb gives him better brain function, but the joint pain is literally unbearable. Other things that he’s tried are every form of probiotic (most recent was the triple therapy by Ruscio) the IgA by Silver Fern, butyrate, etc. and all of these things will give him and day or two of relief and then massive brain fog backlash. He always has to stop using them because the brain fog is so bad.

His stool test yielded results for Streptococcus (that would explain the joint pain) H. pylori and a urine test also showed high sulfur. He clearly has an array of overgrowths going on. He also has been on tons of antibiotics over the years. He treated H. pylori more than once, he had a massive sinus infection for which he went on abx and then had another back slide in his health. He does say that he always feels better for a few days on abx, and then all of his sx’s return with a vengeance.

He has self treated for thyroid issues, and was diagnosed with Hashimoto’s. He’s had a similar result with thyroid where initially he may feel better, but it’s not lasting. He’s currently taking T4 and T3, but given his recent lab results with high TSH and low T3/T4 as well as his low temps, this is clearly not working for him.

He has also been treated for low testosterone and is currently taking bi-weekly shots for this. This has also been another hit or miss situation where sometimes it helps, but other times it does nothing. Given his labs with the high cholesterol and low response to hormone treatment, his liver is clearly limited likely due to the endotoxin from his gut pathogens and overall poor bile flow.

I am heavily suspecting a sulfur issues here, and maybe even high iron (not sure they have been monitoring this even though he’s on T). Not only do all sulfur based things bother him (high fat and protein) taurine, glycine, B vitamins (TTFD etc) choline, etc. the brain fog and digestive ups and downs are also consistent with sulfur metabolism issues. He also had high sulfur metabolites in his urine.

Main problems:

Severe brain fog

Digestive distress: frequent diarrhea with travel

Very low energy and low recovery

“Was diagnosed with H Pylori via GI-Map stool testing. Previously my GI symptoms of gas, bloating, and diarrhea were severe. Have undergone several rounds of antibiotics and supplementation and my GI symptoms have improved, less gas and less bloat. But severe brain fog, especially after eating protein and/or fats, still remains. Still have low energy issues as well.”

History:

Have struggled with GI issues since college (1998-2004). Was able to avoid foods that caused issues and see improvement for many years after graduating. Was in peak physical condition in 2017, then had a bad sinus infection in that took a few rounds of antibiotics to cure. My son was also born at the same time. The lack of sleep from being a new parent, combined with the sinus infections/antibiotics, seemed to set me on my currently list of issues and I haven't been able to get back to my old self since.

I was diagnosed with low testosterone in 2013 (age 34) and have been on hormone replacement therapy since. I was very long on energy then and this helped for many years. But even now it's seems to just barely help me get by.

My body temperate is improved with the thyroid medications, but it seems like I always need to keep increasing my dosage to get the same affects.

I can fall asleep fine but typically wake up after only 4-5 hours, I feel hot and itchy when I do. (another sign of sulfur)

I have tried every probiotic. Many of them help to stabilize my GI issues (less diarrhea, more regular bowel movements, less gas) but they also noticeably make my brain fog much worse.

A low carb diet improves my joint pain/inflammation, and helps me to lean out, but it makes the brain fog and energy levels worse.

Eating higher carbs and lower fat/protein decreases brain fog and helps energy, but my joint pain/inflammation is almost unbearable.

I retain a lot of fat and fluid around my face, even when the rest of my body is relatively lean.

I have a white tongue, I have some dermatitis behind my ears and in my ear canal, that seemed like it could be fungal. An oral swab for Candida was negative and I've tried clotrimazole and it didn't improve those symptoms.

Diet:

7AM breakfast: 2 Eggs and/or some light carbs, such as a croissant

12PM lunch: Typically a chicken salad

6PM dinner: Balanced with lean protein such as steak, chicken, carbs such as white rice or potatoes, vegetables such as carrots or broccoli

Supplements:

Doctor's Best Zinc L-Carnosine, 2 capsules, twice a day with food: Seems to help keep GI symptoms under control Sports Research Vitamin K2, 45 mcg, once a day with breakfast Naturelo Vitamin C, 500mg, once a day with breakfast Thorne Vitamin D, 5,000 IU, once a day with breakfast Thorne Copper Bisglycinate, 2mg, once every other day with breakfast Thorne Calcium Magnesium Malate, 2 x 100mg capsules, once a day with breakfast, helps with energy Con-Cret Creatine HCL Capsules, 1.5g at breakfast and 1.5g at lunch: Helps with energy and brain fog. Too much creatine makes my sleep noticeably worse. Designs for Health Vitamin E, 300mg, once a day with breakfast: Seems to help with thyroid function (increased body temp) Now Vitamin B-1, 100mg, once a day with breakfast: Helps with energy Thorne Vit B3 Niacinaide, 500mg, once a day with breakfast: Helps with energy Thorne Vit B6 P-5-P, 33.8mg, once a day with breakfast: Helps with energy Life Extension Vit B7 Biotin, 600mg, once a day with breakfast: Helps with energy Notes: I have tried all types of magnesium and have trouble absorbing all of them. This one is the best I can tolerate, but more then this dosage and I have diarrhea issues. My GI tract also cannot handle very high Vitamin B complex supplements, especially those high in B12 (another sign of sulfur issues). I've had much better results with the individual ones I'm currently using.

Plan:

I’d really like some lab work here. A full iron panel so we can see serum iron, ferritin, transferrin, etc. Should we get another full thyroid panel with antibodies? He’s been on the current thyroid for over a year and that isn’t doing much for him.

Diet: I am considering a low-sulfur diet for him. I have another client right now where this has been hugely effective. I will take care of this via messenger

Greco Gum: address the recurrent H. Pylori

EGCG: this is helpful for both high liver iron (I’m suspecting this give his sx’s) as well as reducing sulfur bacteria. Designs for Health version

Molybdenum:Mozyme Break this up and nibble 1/3 of the pill with each meal so he’s getting about 50 mcg each time spread throughout the day.

Lactobacillus Rhamnosus GG: this is very effective against H pylori and sulfur bacteria, as well as lactobacillus plantarum from SuperSmart. He will start these one at a time, give one a week to assess before starting another one.

Tributyrin: for gut repopulation/healing.

HMO’s: for gut repopulation

I’m really on the fence here regarding antibiotics. I feel like maybe it would be helpful, but given the amount he’s been given over the years and how negatively this has effected his health I’m more inclined to work on sulfur pathways, reducing iron and repopulating the gut.

---

collagen
